paper_title: Digestion diatom using Battarbee and Ruhland methods for Pengilon Lake, Dieng, Wonosobo, Central Java, Indonesia

methods.description: This method involves the digestion of sediment samples using HCl and H2O2 to prepare diatom samples for analysis. It is based on the Battarbee method and is used to remove organic material from sediment samples.

steps.description: A sediment sample of 1 gram was put into a beaker and given 50 ml of 10% HCl.
steps.searchable_summary: Add 50 ml of 10% HCl to 1 gram of sediment sample in a beaker

steps.description: Sediment samples were heated on a water bath for 2 hours at a temperature of 80-90°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Heat sediment sample in water bath at 80-90°C for 2 hours

steps.description: Sediment samples were deposited for 6-12 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: Allow sediment samples to settle for 6-12 hours

steps.description: The supernatant removed and then added as much as 80 ml of distilled water.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove supernatant and add 80 ml distilled water

steps.description: The 4th treatment was repeated three times; hence the pH was neutral.
steps.searchable_summary: Repeat supernatant removal and water addition until pH is neutral

steps.description: Sediment samples were added with 50 ml of 10% H2O2.
steps.searchable_summary: Add 50 ml of 10% H2O2 to sediment sample

steps.description: Sediment samples were heated for 2 hours at 80-90°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Heat sediment sample with H2O2 at 80-90°C for 2 hours

steps.description: Once heated, and then cooled for 6-12 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: Cool heated sediment sample for 6-12 hours

steps.description: The supernatant removed and then added 80 ml of distilled water. The treatment of the ten repeated three times.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove supernatant and add distilled water, repeat three times

steps.description: Diatom residue dripped over 1 400 ml coverslip, and then placed on a hotplate and heated for 20 minutes and let the adhesive Naphrax.
steps.searchable_summary: Drip diatom residue on coverslip, heat on hotplate for 20 minutes

steps.description: Identification was achieved by distinguishing morphological characteristics similar to taxa, terminology, and identification using Krammer & Lange-Bertalot.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify diatoms using morphological characteristics and Krammer & Lange-Bertalot

methods.description: This method involves the digestion of sediment samples using H2SO4 and HNO3 to prepare diatom samples for analysis. It is based on the Ruhland method and is used to remove organic material from sediment samples.

steps.description: Glass beaker prepared. HNO3 5.3 ml put in a glass beaker advance slowly.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare glass beaker and add 5.3 ml HNO3 slowly

steps.description: 4.7 ml H2SO4 was added to the beaker slowly and while stirring using a stirrer.
steps.searchable_summary: Add 4.7 ml H2SO4 to beaker while stirring

steps.description: The acid mixture is gradually poured into the sediment in each glass and then stirred (2-5 ml, 5-7 ml, and 7-10 ml).
steps.searchable_summary: Gradually pour acid mixture into sediment and stir

steps.description: Then the digestion process is the same as the Battarbee method.
steps.searchable_summary: Follow Battarbee method steps for digestion
